articles.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
articles.summary: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

articles.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
articles.summary: Donald Trump pays tribute to the 71-year-old South Carolina senator, calling him a "true American patriot" who will be "greatly missed".

articles.title: Badly burned British couple rescued from ravine during Spain wildfires, reports say
articles.summary: The unnamed pair were found semi-conscious with 40% burns after being trapped by the blazes raging through Almeria province, local media reports.

articles.title: China evacuates nearly two million people as powerful typhoon makes landfall
articles.summary: Schools, work and outdoor activities have been suspended in Zhejiang province and many transport services cancelled.

articles.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
articles.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

articles.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
articles.summary: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

articles.title: David Willey, esteemed BBC Vatican correspondent, dies aged 93
articles.summary: One of the BBC's longest-serving foreign correspondents, he reported on five popes during his career.

articles.title: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting
articles.summary: Police say there was an exchange of gunfire between two individuals at the festival, and two firearms had been recovered from the scene.

articles.title: Muslim judge in India faces death threats after convicting 'cow vigilantes'
articles.summary: Tabassum Khan has been facing online abuse after convicting 14 Hindu men in a lynching case.

articles.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
articles.summary: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.
